records.ety: [F. symptme, Gr. <?/ anything that has befallen one, a chance, causality, symptom, fr. <?/ to fall together; sy`n with + <?/ to fall; akin to Skr. pat to fly, to fall. See Syn-, and cf. Asymptote, Feather.]

defs: Any affection which accompanies disease; a perceptible change in the body or its functions, which indicates disease, or the kind or phases of disease; as, the causes of disease often lie beyond our sight, but we learn their nature by the symptoms exhibited.
quotes: Like the sick man, we are expiring with all sorts of good symptoms.

defs: A sign or token; that which indicates the existence of something else; as, corruption in elections is a symptom of the decay of public virtue.
